<!--
TITLE GUIDANCE — the PR title states the plain-language OUTCOME, not the mechanism.
  Good:  "Owners can see what Alloro fixed on their Google profile"
  Not:   "Add MetricAction rail + surface it on the stage reader"

Fill every section below. Keep the five headers. A PR the reviewer cannot situate in plain
language — what it is, where it's seen, who it touches — is returned, not reviewed (protocol Rule 3).
The italic instruction lines are guidance; replace them with your content.
-->

## What this is
*2-3 sentences a non-engineer reads once: what was wrong or missing, what this makes true.*

## Where you see it
*Exact surface and path (page + URL), or "no visible surface" plus what it feeds.*

## Who it touches
*Named clients/orgs affected, funnel gate (Get Found / Considered / Chosen), tracker item, and any PR this depends on or composes with.*

## Planned action after merge
*Dev validation, wire-up, backfill, pending decision, or dormant-behind-switch.*

## Acceptance
*Numbered T-items per protocol Rule 3; every claim in this body must map to a passing item.*
